skillEvidence.evidence: comfortable with study design basics, bias/confounding, cohort definitions
wrote short reports for program managers

background.resume: I have 3 years of experience in public health and epidemiology. I worked on a county health department analytics team where I cleaned survey and registry data, summarized trends, and wrote short reports for program managers. One project I led was a vaccination access analysis combining appointment data with census tract demographics.

artifacts.text:
Role: Real World Evidence Analyst
Company: Meridian Therapeutics
Responsibilities:
- Support observational research projects using claims, EHR, and registry datasets
- Translate clinical questions into cohort definitions, index dates, inclusion/exclusion criteria, outcomes, and covariates
- Document assumptions, limitations, and data-quality concerns clearly
- Create concise evidence summaries for medical affairs and HEOR stakeholders

target.instructions:
INTERVIEW INTAKE
Company: Meridian Therapeutics
Round step: First round
Interviewer kind: Engineer / peer
Format: Video
Seniority, in their words: Same level, new field
Their stated challenge: I freeze when they ask why I have not worked in pharma.
Interviewers (pasted by the user): Alex Chen — Senior RWE engineer: works on claims data quality and reproducible cohort pipelines.
Signals — content fears: claims cohort definition
Signals — personal obstacles: freezing under pressure
Signals — tone: gentle
DIAGNOSTIC RESULTS (build the map FROM these):
Q1: substance=met (clear public-health bridge) structure=thin (buried the result)
Q2: substance=thin (named cohort rules but missed limitations) structure=met (answer first)

responsibilities: Support observational research projects using claims, EHR, and registry datasets
Translate clinical questions into cohort definitions, index dates, inclusion/exclusion criteria, outcomes, and covariates
Document assumptions, limitations, and data-quality concerns clearly
Create concise evidence summaries for medical affairs and HEOR stakeholders
